prompt: I have a Next.js e-commerce app at /home/seif/test-ecommerce. The checkout flow user story is: 'As a customer, I can add items to my cart, enter shipping info, choose a payment method, and complete my purchase.' I need comprehensive Playwright E2E tests for this. The app runs on port 3000.
assertions: Tests are organized in describe blocks by behavior (happy path, validation, error handling, etc.)
Happy path test covers the full flow: add to cart -> shipping -> payment -> order confirmation
Validation tests cover empty required fields and invalid inputs
Boundary value tests exist for at least one numeric/string field
External payment API is mocked using page.route()
All locators use semantic methods (getByRole, getByLabel, getByText, getByTestId) — no CSS selectors or XPath
All assertions use web-first auto-retrying methods (toBeVisible, toHaveText, etc.) — no manual isVisible() checks
Tests are independent — no shared state between tests
At least one accessibility test is included (axe-core or keyboard navigation)
A test plan or coverage summary is presented before writing code
